domain_experience: senior data analyst at an ecommerce SaaS company
direct exposure to merchant reporting pain
experience translating reporting requests into practical analysis
assets: pattern knowledge of merchant reporting complaints
familiarity with common ecommerce reporting workflows
ability to spot trust-breaking analytics issues quickly
distribution: peer network useful for interviews and referrals
likely warm access to operators and analytics peers
network_access: limited direct access to budget owners
constraints: uncertain whether to build micro-SaaS immediately
likely limited time outside primary job
credibility_signals: domain fluency in ecommerce reporting pain
unfair_advantages: better understanding of real merchant reporting pain than a generalist builder
can recognize recurring high-value reporting workflows quickly
can sell outcomes around trust and decision speed rather than generic dashboards

opportunities.title: Ecommerce reporting audit plus implementation sprint
opportunities.thesis: Sell a fixed-scope audit and cleanup sprint that identifies high-cost reporting blind spots and implements a cleaner KPI pack.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user already knows the recurring merchant reporting failure modes and can diagnose them faster than a general consultant.
validation_plan: Test a fixed-scope audit offer with warm contacts
Run 3-5 pain interviews to refine the promise
Ask for one paid cleanup sprint
kill_criteria: No clear willingness to pay for a one-time audit
Every prospect needs fully custom project work

opportunities.title: Fractional merchant analytics and reporting concierge
opportunities.thesis: Offer a recurring monthly service that gives ecommerce operators clean KPI reporting, anomaly explanation, and fast answers without a full-time hire.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This is closest to the user's current edge because it monetizes real workflow knowledge immediately and fits warm peer-driven distribution.
validation_plan: Interview operators and agencies about recurring reporting pain
Package a monthly concierge offer with fixed deliverables
kill_criteria: Prospects like the idea but will not pay for an outside reporting partner
The work cannot be scoped into a repeatable monthly service

opportunities.title: Micro-SaaS for merchant KPI trust and anomaly explanation
opportunities.thesis: Build a narrow tool that unifies a few core data sources, flags KPI anomalies, and explains likely causes in plain English.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user understands the reporting questions that matter, but current distribution and buyer access are too weak to make software-first the best opening move.
validation_plan: Validate the recurring anomaly-explanation pain through interviews
Test the workflow manually before building customer-facing software
Only build after repeated paid usage appears
kill_criteria: No repeated demand for the same monitoring workflow
Prospects view it as a nice-to-have dashboard rather than urgent workflow support

opportunities.title: Ecommerce benchmark feed or KPI template subscription
opportunities.thesis: Sell recurring templates, scorecards, and eventually benchmark comparisons for merchants by segment.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can define useful reporting structures, though the moat is weak without proprietary data or distribution.
validation_plan: Test willingness to pay for templates before benchmarking
Avoid benchmark claims until data depth exists
kill_criteria: Low willingness to pay for templates alone
No path to proprietary benchmark data

opportunities.title: Reporting concierge to internal automation to micro-SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Start with a productized reporting concierge, automate the repeated internal steps, and turn the most common workflow into a narrow software product.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This best exploits the user's reporting-domain edge while matching current access constraints and improving odds of a later micro-SaaS.
validation_plan: Sell one audit plus one recurring concierge pilot
Productize only after 3 customers share the same core job
kill_criteria: No paid service demand from warm network and referrals
No repeated workflow emerges across multiple customers
The work stays highly bespoke and cannot be narrowed

next_actions: Write a one-page reporting audit plus monthly concierge offer
Run 10 warm discovery conversations with operators, agencies, and analytics peers
Test a paid pilot before building any customer-facing SaaS
Track which workflow steps repeat across prospects and pilots
Only build software around the narrowest repeated high-frequency pain
